model_narrative: We plan a single round trip that visits a set of cities. For every ordered pair of cities we decide whether the trip travels directly from the first to the second. Each direct leg has a known travel cost. The objective is to make the total travel cost of the chosen route as small as possible.

sets.name: I
sets.doc: the cities that must all be visited on the tour

sets.name: N
sets.doc: an index over every nonempty proper subset of the cities; each member names one such subset (sizes one through the number of cities minus one). The subsets are enumerated by taking, for every size from one up to one less than the total number of cities, all combinations of cities of that size in the natural order of the city list, and naming them n1, n2, and so on in that enumeration order. A constraint is written once per subset

params.name: c
params.doc: the travel cost of going directly from the first city to the second city of the pair; the diagonal (a city to itself) is zero

vars.name: x
vars.doc: one when the tour goes directly from the first city to the second city of the pair, zero otherwise; the diagonal entries (a city to itself) are fixed to zero so no city links to itself

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total travel cost of the chosen tour

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Every city must have exactly one outgoing direct leg. For each city, the legs that leave it to go directly to some city must together add up to exactly one.

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Every city must have exactly one incoming direct leg. For each city, the legs that arrive at it coming directly from some city must together add up to exactly one.

constraints.name: z
constraints.intent: The total travel cost is the sum over every ordered pair of cities of that pair's travel cost counted only when the trip uses the direct leg between them. Set the total cost equal to this sum.

constraints.name: I)
constraints.intent: No proper group of cities may form its own closed loop separate from the rest of the route. For each such group of cities, the number of chosen direct legs that both start and end inside the group must be no more than one fewer than the number of cities in the group.

query: Roadworks are about to close the link between city 4 and city 5 in both directions for the season. With that connection unavailable, what does the best route cost?

query: We're looking at a scenario where the depot at city 6 loses its direct connections to both city 1 and city 2 — no leg may run between 6 and either of them in either direction. What does the best tour come to then?

query: Suppose the direct road between city 2 and city 3 is taken out of service both ways. Drop that connection and tell me how the route distance shifts.

query: The route threads straight through the whole 1-2-3 cluster, chaining all three neighbours back to back. Wouldn't it be healthier to break that up and use at most one leg inside the 1-2-3 group? What's the model seeing that makes it want to chain them?
expected_pyomo: model.spread_123 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[a, b] for a in ['i1', 'i2', 'i3'] for b in ['i1', 'i2', 'i3']) <= 1)

query: The plan keeps leaning on the direct shortcut between city 1 and city 3. That feels like a single point of failure — couldn't we serve the loop without ever using a direct 1-3 leg? What's the trade-off keeping it on that shortcut?
